# Story 1 Final — Document graph: visible edges

> **Codex review unavailable.** Codex harness was dispatched twice and hung at 0
> bytes for >5min each time. Final plan below merges the original plan with a
> fresh-eyes audit performed directly against the codebase.

## Diagnosis

Original plan diagnosis confirmed by direct file inspection:

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte:107` —
  arrow marker uses `fill-border-line dark:fill-dark-border-line`
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.svelte:165` —
  edge `<line>` uses `stroke-border-line dark:stroke-dark-border-line`
- `tailwind.config.js` confirms `border-line: #E4DDD0` vs `bg-canvas: #F4EFE6`
  (~1.05:1 contrast) and `dark-border-line: #34302A` vs `dark-bg-canvas: #1A1815`
  (~1.4:1) — far below WCAG AA 3:1 for non-text graphics.
- Stroke width 1.5, opacity 0.6 compound the issue.
- Labels at line 187 already use `fill-fg-muted dark:fill-dark-fg-muted` —
  proposed token is proven to compile through the same generator.

Audit additions:

- **Sole consumer**: `D3Graph.svelte` is imported in exactly one place:
  `routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/+page.svelte:6`. No bead
  graph, run graph, storybook usage, or other consumers. Class swap is fully
  scoped.
- **No existing graph e2e**: no `graph.e2e.ts` exists; sibling pattern
  (`drain.e2e.ts`, `personas.e2e.ts`) is established and `playwright.config.ts`
  is already wired.

## Final bead list

1. **bead-A** — `fix(web): doc graph edges and arrowheads use foreground token, not border-line`
   - Surface: `D3Graph.svelte` lines 107, 165 only.
   - Change: swap `border-line` -> `fg-muted` on edge stroke and arrow marker
     fill; bump `stroke-width` 1.5 -> 1.75 and `stroke-opacity` 0.6 -> 0.9;
     add `stroke-linecap="round"`.
   - ACs: AC1, AC2, AC3, AC4, AC6, AC7.
   - Tier: **cheap** (deterministic ~6-line edit).
   - Deps: none.

2. **bead-B** — `test(web): e2e regression check for doc graph edge color tokens`
   - Surface: new
     `routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/graph.e2e.ts`.
   - Assertions: `<line>` count >= 1; computed `stroke` resolves to
     `rgb(75, 85, 99)` (light `fg-muted` #4B5563); marker `path` fill matches;
     repeat after toggling `dark` class on `<html>` to verify `#B8AF9C`.
   - Tier: **standard** (Playwright theme toggle + GraphQL fixture wiring).
   - Deps: bead-A.

3. **bead-C** — `docs(spec): FEAT-008 AC for graph edge contrast (WCAG AA non-text)`
   - Surface: `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md` only.
   - Tier: **cheap**.
   - Deps: none. Can land before/parallel/after bead-A.

## Tier suggestions

- bead-A: cheap (qwen-class) — pure class swap, easy review.
- bead-B: standard (sonnet-class) — needs theme toggle pattern + selectors;

      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)

Update a bead

Usage:
  ddx bead update <id> [flags]

Flags:
      --acceptance string    New acceptance criteria
      --assignee string      New assignee or claim assignee fallback
      --claim                Claim: set status=in_progress, assignee=ddx
      --description string   New description
  -h, --help                 help for update
      --labels string        New labels (comma-separated)
      --notes string         New notes
      --parent string        New parent bead ID
      --priority int         New priority
      --set stringArray      Set custom field (key=value, repeatable)
      --status string        New status (open, in_progress, closed)
      --title string         New title
      --unclaim              Unclaim: set status=open, clear assignee and claim fields
      --unset stringArray    Unset custom field (key repeatable)

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

input.command:
set -e
PARENT=ddx-b18d9ce6
RUNDIR=.ddx/executions/20260502T113639-9008e965
> /tmp/child-ids.txt
while IFS='|' read -r N TITLE; do
  PLAN="$RUNDIR/story-plans/story-$N-final.md"
  DESC="Story $N of Phase 2 umbrella ($PARENT). Final implementation plan committed at $PLAN — read it before starting; it contains the diagnosis, sub-bead breakdown (bead-A/B/C…), tier suggestions, and locked decisions. Decompose into the listed sub-beads (use \`ddx bead create\` with this bead as parent and \`ddx bead dep add\`) if the work doesn't fit a single attempt."
  ACC="Sub-beads enumerated in $PLAN are filed (or implemented) and closed. All ACs in the plan's 'AC mapping' / final bead list are met. ddx doc audit passes on any new specs/research artifacts."
  LABELS="phase:2,story:$N"
  ID=$(ddx bead create "Story $N — $TITLE" --type epic --parent "$PARENT" --labels "$LABELS" --description "$DESC" --acceptance "$ACC" 2>&1 | tee /tmp/create.out | grep -oE 'ddx-[0-9a-f]+' | head -1)
  if [ -z "$ID" ]; then echo "FAILED story $N:"; cat /tmp/create.out; exit 1; fi
  echo "$N $ID" >> /tmp/child-ids.txt
  echo "Created story $N: $ID"
done < /tmp/titles.txt
echo "---"
cat /tmp/child-ids.txt

Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>   </governing>   <instructions> You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check (do this first)

Before writing any code, decide whether this bead is sized for a single bounded-context attempt. Coarse signals that the bead is too big:

- More than ~6 acceptance criteria spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables rather than a single coherent change.
- Description explicitly lists multiple feature-sized pieces ("AC4 is a tier-inference engine", "AC5 introduces a new status type").
- You estimate more than ~500 lines of meaningful code change across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If the bead is too big, do NOT attempt the work. Instead, in this single attempt:

1. Use the `bash` tool to run `ddx bead create` to file child beads, one per coherent slice of work. Pass each child a focused title, description, and acceptance criteria; copy labels and `spec-id` from the parent.
2. Use `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record the dependency edge.
3. Append a short note to the parent (`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`) so future runs see why no implementation landed.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260502T113639-9008e965/no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining the decomposition: list each child bead ID and the slice it covers, and stop.

A clean decomposition that produces tractable children is a successful attempt. Do not push partial implementation alongside decomposition.

If the bead IS sized for one attempt, continue with the rest of these instructions.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, writing `.ddx/executions/20260502T113639-9008e965/no_changes_rationale.txt` is mandatory before you exit. Include: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. If you exit without a commit and without this file, DDx records `no_evidence_produced` and treats the attempt as a harness failure, not as legitimate no_changes. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## Investigation and report outputs

If the bead asks you to "output a report", "document findings", or otherwise produce a freestanding artifact that is not source code, write it under `.ddx/executions/20260502T113639-9008e965/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — paths outside the repo are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and do not survive between machines, which causes the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/20260502T113639-9008e965/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside any code changes so it lands in the merge.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.   </instructions> </execute-bead>

- You estimate more than ~500 lines of meaningful code change across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If the bead is too big, do NOT attempt the work. Instead, in this single attempt:

1. Use the `bash` tool to run `ddx bead create` to file child beads, one per coherent slice of work. Pass each child a focused title, description, and acceptance criteria; copy labels and `spec-id` from the parent.
2. Use `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record the dependency edge.
3. Append a short note to the parent (`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`) so future runs see why no implementation landed.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260502T113725-7f3f409d/no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining the decomposition: list each child bead ID and the slice it covers, and stop.

A clean decomposition that produces tractable children is a successful attempt. Do not push partial implementation alongside decomposition.

If the bead IS sized for one attempt, continue with the rest of these instructions.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, writing `.ddx/executions/20260502T113725-7f3f409d/no_changes_rationale.txt` is mandatory before you exit. Include: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. If you exit without a commit and without this file, DDx records `no_evidence_produced` and treats the attempt as a harness failure, not as legitimate no_changes. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## Investigation and report outputs

If the bead asks you to "output a report", "document findings", or otherwise produce a freestanding artifact that is not source code, write it under `.ddx/executions/20260502T113725-7f3f409d/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — paths outside the repo are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and do not survive between machines, which causes the reviewer to BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/20260502T113725-7f3f409d/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside any code changes so it lands in the merge.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.   </instructions> </execute-bead>

input.content:
Bead: ddx-b18d9ce6 — Phase 2: post-redesign UX, ops, topology, autonomy
Attempt: 20260502T113639-9008e965

Outcome: no_changes (already decomposed)

What is done
============
The umbrella epic ddx-b18d9ce6 has already been decomposed into 18 story-level
child epics (Stories 1-18 from the 2026-05 redesign batch). Every Story epic
already lists ddx-b18d9ce6 as its parent. The acceptance criteria for the
umbrella ("All N sub-epics closed") cannot be satisfied by code in this
attempt — it is satisfied only when the child epics close.

The 18 story-level children, all parent=ddx-b18d9ce6:

  ddx-db5e0227  Story 1:  doc graph visible edges
  ddx-bc346d44  Story 2:  doc graph dark mode visibility
  ddx-86ccbb75  Story 3:  doc graph force-directed layout (no overlap)
  ddx-781af038  Story 4:  doc viewer scope to docs/ + dedupe scanner
  ddx-ffb678fc  Story 5:  artifact viewer categorization by metadata
  ddx-4728ae0f  Story 6:  artifact listing search/sort/filter
  ddx-97335425  Story 7:  artifact listing scale to thousands
  ddx-723d93f2  Story 8:  workers/runs tabs reorganization
  ddx-12e97cb9  Story 9:  agent availability tab
  ddx-e34994e2  Story 10: automatic escalation + always-review
  ddx-64b9a198  Story 11: aggregations on availability page
  ddx-a61bf8ee  Story 12: optimize ddx run/try/work prompts
  ddx-921616ea  Story 13: metric artifact type
  ddx-a038a090  Story 14: server coordinator + clients (multi-node)
  ddx-1d85c927  Story 15: server-side prompt input
  ddx-a8a18cb7  Story 16: expose server task session logs for review
  ddx-43d67aa5  Story 17: artifact-type template/example/prompt panel
  ddx-42b917fe  Story 18: interactive artifact review (template + chat)

(Note: the umbrella description says "17-story batch"; the queue contains 18
story epics tagged phase:2 — Story 18 was filed alongside the original 17.
The acceptance criterion "All 17 sub-epics closed" is read here as "all
phase:2 story epics under this parent close.")

Each story epic in turn has its own filed sub-beads (search labels
`phase:2,story:N`) covering area:web/server/specs/tests/etc. Those are the
work units the drain consumes; the umbrella has no implementation surface
of its own.

What is blocking
================
Nothing structural. The umbrella is an aggregator. It will close once the
18 child story epics close, which depends on the drain working through their
sub-beads.

What a follow-up attempt would need
===================================
Do not re-attempt the umbrella as an implementation bead. Two valid future
moves:

  1. Wait for the drain to close all 18 child story epics, then close
     ddx-b18d9ce6 with `ddx bead close ddx-b18d9ce6` (manual or via a
     "close-when-children-closed" sweep).
  2. If a future tool gains "auto-close umbrella when all children close"
     behavior, ddx-b18d9ce6 will close automatically.
